learningObjectives.title: Understand package building profile
learningObjectives.description: Explain how package building rules shape handling units before transportation planning.

learningObjectives.title: Understand package strategies
learningObjectives.description: Identify pallet, container and mixed-package approaches for planning optimization.

learningObjectives.title: Understand planning impact
learningObjectives.description: Describe effects of packaging quality on loading, capacity and freight optimization.

sections.content: A Package Building Profile (PBP) defines how products are packed into handling units before transportation planning begins. It specifies packaging rules, capacity limits and packaging hierarchy, allowing SAP TM to optimize loading, resource utilization and freight planning.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: SAP TM plans transportation based on packaged goods. The Package Building Profile determines how products are grouped into handling units before Freight Units are planned.

A beverage company ships 2,400 bottles to a distributor. SAP TM uses the Package Building Profile to pack 24 bottles into one carton, 40 cartons onto one pallet and multiple pallets into a truck. Transportation planning is performed using these handling units rather than individual bottles.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Maintain realistic package dimensions and weight limits. Accurate package data significantly improves transportation optimization and vehicle loading.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A consumer goods company introduced Package Building Profiles for palletization. Vehicle utilization increased by 22% because SAP TM planned shipments using optimized pallet configurations instead of individual cartons.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Package Building Profiles are frequently discussed in advanced SAP TM interviews. Remember that package building occurs before Freight Unit planning and directly influences capacity planning.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not confuse Package Building Profiles with Loading Profiles. Package Building defines how products are packed, whereas loading determines how packages are arranged inside transportation resources.

items: Package Building Profiles define packaging rules.
Handling Units are generated before transportation planning.
Package Building is a key master data object in SAP TM.
